exam_rules.market: Indian market (Indian brands or MNCs operating in India)
exam_rules.global_branding_exception: For global branding questions, examples must be Indian/MNC brands going abroad (NOT about multinationals entering India)

citation_format.note: If multiple references in one answer, assign a number to each reference and cite the number next to the copied sentence. List all references with numbers at the end of the answer.

professor_patterns.trick_questions: Questions may appear theoretical but always require real, cited examples from the Indian market. Global branding questions flip the expectation — examples must be Indian/MNC brands going ABROAD, not MNCs entering India.
professor_patterns.common_examples_penalized: Overused or commonly cited brands (e.g., Amul, Maggi) are penalized even if not explicitly banned. If the same example is used by many students, all of them score poorly. Uniqueness is actively rewarded.
professor_patterns.data_required_for_high_marks: Wherever possible, include numbers and data to make the answer look credible. However, revenue/growth/profit figures are NOT required to justify success or failure unless explicitly asked — focus on whether the example is innovative.
professor_patterns.concept_precision: All answers must be grounded in the reading material's concepts. If departing from the author's view, the student must first state the author's view clearly before presenting their own argument.
professor_patterns.proof_of_success: Copy-paste the exact words/sentences from the reference that serve as evidence directly into the answer in quotes. References that don't match or support the claim attract penalties.
professor_patterns.all_parts_must_be_addressed: Answers must confine to the space provided and the word limit. Exceeding the word limit is stiffly penalized. There will be severe time pressure — stay focused on the question.
professor_patterns.cite_page_paragraph_for_book: When referencing or critiquing a theory from the textbook, cite the exact page number AND paragraph number. Without this, fair evaluation is not possible.
professor_patterns.banned_brand_zero: Using any banned brand (explicitly listed or discussed in class/presentations) results in zero points for that entire answer, with no exceptions.
professor_patterns.chatgpt_answers_known: The evaluators already have ChatGPT-generated answers for all likely questions. Copying or paraphrasing ChatGPT responses is detected and penalized. Original thinking and unique examples are the only reliable path to high marks.

senior_advice.strategy: First understand the concept (e.g., via NotebookLM), then match a pre-prepared unique example, then find a supporting article online and cite it.
senior_advice.textbook_index_useful: If NotebookLM fails, use the textbook keyword index at the back to find the relevant section.
